# Sinux - Signal-driven state management

## Overview
Sinux is a state management library for React implementing Facebook Flux architecture with self-dispatching signals.

## Packages
- @sinuxjs/core: Store, Signal, createStore, computed, computedFrom, middlewares (persist, devtools, immer)
- @sinuxjs/react: useStore, useStores, useComputed, combine
- @sinuxjs/tanstack-query: querySignal, mutationSignal, tanstackQuery middleware
- @sinuxjs/apollo: graphqlQuery, graphqlMutation, apolloClient middleware

## Key Concepts
- Stores hold state and expose named signal methods
- Signals are async commands that update state: store.mySignal(args) -> Promise<Partial<T>>
- Middleware intercepts dispatches: onInit, onDispatch, onStateChange
- React hooks use useSyncExternalStore for concurrent-safe subscriptions

## Links
- Docs: /docs/intro
- Getting Started: /docs/getting-started
- GitHub: https://github.com/jbpin/sinux
